rationale: Load-bearing files and symbols that anchor TokenKey's fifth platform `newapi`. The fifth-platform integration was silently regressed multiple times by upstream merges before this guard existed. If any sentinel below is missing, the platform either silently disappears from the UI/scheduling pool or fails at runtime. This file is the single source of truth read by both `scripts/preflight.sh` (newapi sentinel registry section, always) and `.github/workflows/upstream-merge-pr-shape.yml` (newapi sentinels check, on `merge/upstream-*` PRs). Adding a new load-bearing surface for newapi MUST add a sentinel here in the same commit.

sentinels.rationale: Identity constant for the fifth platform string `newapi`. Removing it collapses every 5-platform invariant in the codebase.

TK companion that partitions the OpenAI-compat scheduling pool by `group.platform`. Without it, scheduling falls back to bare `IsOpenAI()` and silently excludes all newapi accounts (the original P0 regression that triggered docs/approved/newapi-as-fifth-platform.md).
TK companion that fetches candidate accounts using the group's actual platform instead of hardcoded `PlatformOpenAI`. Without it, `group.platform=newapi` requests get an empty pool.

Shared sticky-key ingress parser for prod-to-edge NewAPI/Anthropic forwarding. Without this helper, prod-injected X-Session-Id can drift from DeriveStickyKey priority and edge falls back to volatile message-content hashes.

ParsedRequest must preserve explicit sticky identity and top-level prompt_cache_key so the Anthropic edge ingress can consume prod-forwarded sticky signals without reparsing or falling back to message content; request correlation IDs are also load-bearing inputs for sticky-source evidence indexing. StripCountTokensUnsupportedFields + its declarative field list are TK defensive sanitize for the Anthropic count_tokens endpoint (strips temperature/top_p/top_k/stop_sequences/max_tokens/stream/metadata/service_tier/context_management that Anthropic rejects with `Extra inputs are not permitted`). `context_management` MUST stay in the list because the OAuth mimic path (normalizeClaudeOAuthRequestBody) actively injects it for Sonnet/Opus thinking on /v1/messages; without it, count_tokens 400s every OAuth request (2026-05-18 09:10:30 prod trace). Dropping any symbol regresses the 2026-05-18 prod incident where claude-cli leaked `temperature` into count_tokens and the resulting 400 burst tripped the per-account upstream-error breaker (see gateway_service.go sentinel for the matching call sites + 400 exemption + post-normalize final-strip).

Anthropic GenerateSessionHash must prefer metadata.user_id, then explicit headers, then prompt_cache_key before the message-content fallback. Losing this priority recreates the max_sessions exhaustion incident on edge OAuth accounts. The sticky-source sink event emission is also load-bearing for request-id based production debugging.

Anthropic handlers share this TK companion to populate group, explicit sticky headers, and context before account selection. Without the shared hook, /v1/messages or count_tokens can skip the prod-forwarded sticky identity. Request correlation IDs are part of the same load-bearing contract for sticky-source observability evidence.
TK companion that decides whether `messages_dispatch_model_config` is preserved. Without it, newapi groups have their dispatch config silently wiped on save.

TK companion invoked from admin_service.go CreateAccount/UpdateAccount that pins newapi/Moonshot accounts to the regional base_url their API key actually accepts. Without it, admins silently save accounts with the wrong region (api.moonshot.cn vs .ai), and the relay hot path 401s every request because it deliberately does not do per-request region fallback. Build-break alone is insufficient: an upstream merge that simultaneously removes the call sites would silently regress this without compile failure.

Channel-type catalog backing the fifth-platform admin UI. The integration package is the entire fifth-platform glue layer; if this file disappears the catalog dropdown is empty and bridge selection breaks.

Bridge for the asynchronous video task pipeline (POST /v1/video/generations + GET /v1/video/generations/:task_id and the OpenAI-compat /v1/videos aliases) — currently the only newapi pathway for VolcEngine / DoubaoVideo video generation. Without these symbols the gateway routes silently 5xx because no other code drives task adaptors. An upstream merge that drops this file regresses every Volcengine video customer.

Service-side interface (VideoTaskCache) + DTO (VideoTaskRecord) for the video task registry. Concrete impl lives in repository/video_task_cache.go (Redis-backed; depguard forbids service from importing redis directly). Removing this file collapses the entire video-fetch path: handler can't compile, dispatch helper can't compile, registry routing pin disappears.

Repository-side concrete impl of service.VideoTaskCache. Two implementations: redis-backed for production (single source of truth across replicas) and in-memory for unit tests (rdb=nil). NewVideoTaskCache is the constructor wired in repository/wire.go. An upstream merge that drops this file silently 404s every video task GET because no impl is registered.

TK companion that wires the bridge's video submit/fetch into OpenAIGatewayService. Endpoint eligibility now flows through backend/internal/engine/* via BuildDispatchPlan + BridgeEndpointEnabled, so the old tkBridgeEndpointEnabled helper is intentionally gone. These symbols still guard the load-bearing video submit/fetch bridge entry points; removing them silently regresses async video routing for newapi-backed channels.
Bridge fusion logic that wires QuantumNous/new-api adaptors into TK's relay. Removing this file disables the entire upstream new-api capability.
Endpoint resolution must list `newapi` in the OpenAI-compat case branch. Without it, gateway endpoint inference for newapi groups returns empty strings and routing fails.
Canonical 5-platform enumeration consumed by every admin UI picker, filter, and segmented control. Dropping `newapi` here silently removes the platform from every selector.
Shared composable that builds platform option lists for admin pickers/filters. The `newapi:` label entry is what surfaces the fifth platform's display name.

NewAPI upstream model discovery changed in #128 from string[] to object records that carry pricing_status. The frontend API wrapper must normalize both old and new response shapes and preserve pricing status metadata; otherwise fetched objects flow into account forms and crash string-only UI paths before create/update reaches the backend.

The NewAPI account composable is the contract boundary between #128's object response and the account submit payload. It must split fetched models into string IDs for allowedModels/model_mapping and a separate pricing-status map for display-only metadata; if an upstream merge reverts this, create/edit silently breaks with object values in string-only code. PR #160 also fixed two silent data-loss paths: model_pricing_status must be persisted in credentials, and old accounts that lack it must be able to refresh pricing-only metadata without replacing their saved whitelist/model_mapping.

The NewAPI create branch is a direct doCreateAccount path rather than the generic createAccountAndFinish helper. PR #160 fixed a production data-loss bug where newapi account creation dropped apikey quota extra, leaving tokensea-1/2 with extra={}. These literals keep the direct newapi payload wired to the same quota-extra builder as other apikey/bedrock creates.

Existing NewAPI accounts created before model_pricing_status persistence have no pricing metadata in credentials. EditAccountModal must silently refresh pricing-only metadata from stored credentials so badges render without overwriting the saved whitelist/model_mapping. This is easy to lose in upstream modal rewrites because the form still compiles and saves without it.

The shared NewAPI field component must pass #128 pricing_status metadata into the whitelist selector without changing submit semantics. Dropping this binding compiles cleanly but regresses the pricing-source-of-truth UI contract.

The selector is the UI crash point from the production incident: object-shaped model entries from #128 must be normalized to IDs before includes/render paths, while pricing_status remains badge metadata only. Upstream merges touching this shared component can revert to string[] assumptions without compile failures.

ModelIcon was one of the observed crash sites (`model.toLowerCase is not a function`). It must tolerate object-shaped model values and derive icon matching from a normalized string, because upstream/shared UI changes can reintroduce direct string assumptions while TypeScript still compiles at call sites.

buildModelMappingObject must tolerate stale object entries and emit only string-to-string model_mapping pairs. Without this normalization, the submit path can hit `includes is not a function` or persist invalid object-shaped model mappings after #128 responses. This file also owns the admin default model lists; keep Claude defaults on currently available 4.x models and do not re-add retired 3.7 Sonnet entries that trigger Anthropic not_found errors.

Read-side predicate consumed by integration/newapi/discover_filter_tk.go step [3] to tag admin 'fetch upstream models' results with pricing_status. OpenRouter / Azure / Bedrock report '<vendor>/<family-version>' ids whose tail uses '.' while the LiteLLM catalog stores bare names with '-' and date suffixes (anthropic/claude-opus-4.5 vs claude-opus-4-5-20251001). stripVendorPrefixForCatalogLookup + the '.'→'-' normalization is what keeps the entire OpenRouter / Azure model list from being tagged 'missing' across admin and the client /v1/models filter. Removing either symbol silently flips ~all OpenRouter channels to a wall of orange 'missing' badges (the regression this sentinel guards).
